findings.title: Hindi speech is now silently dropped whenever the backend is connected â€” including the Hindi half of the emergency phrase
findings.scenario: Patient has showHindi enabled. They trigger EMERGENCY (GlobalNavBar.tsx:195-201 builds `${enPhrase} ... ${hiPhrase}` with Devanagari text). With the backend connected (the normal full-app state), handleSpeak now routes ONLY to ws.speak -> pyttsx3/SAPI5, which speaks the English half and emits literally zero audio for the Hindi half â€” the caregiver never hears the Hindi call for help. Any pure-Hindi phrase (PhrasesScreen / QuickFires Hindi entries, Hindi keyboard text, ttsLanguage='hindi' or 'auto') is now TOTAL SILENCE when connected. Before this commit, every utterance also went through browser speechSynthesis with u.lang='hi-IN' (App.tsx:149-156, including Devanagari auto-detect), which selects a Hindi voice whenever one is installed â€” on a rig with a Hindi voice pack, Hindi worked; after this commit it cannot, regardless of installed voices.
findings.evidence: ws.speak is `(text) => send('speak', { text })` (useWebSocket.tsx:608) â€” the protocol carries NO language, rate, or volume. The backend handler is `'speak': lambda: self._speak(data.get('text', ''))` (main.py:1860) and TTSEngine._init_engine (main.py:686-705) pins one voice at startup ('prefer female' name match) with zero language switching anywhere in main.py (grep for 'hindi' in python/main.py: no hits). Empirically verified on this machine: SAPI5 (the exact engine pyttsx3 uses) synthesizing the Hindi emergency phrase 'à¤®à¥à¤à¥‡ à¤¤à¥à¤°à¤‚à¤¤ à¤®à¤¦à¤¦ à¤šà¤¾à¤¹à¤¿à¤' produced a 46-byte WAV (header only, zero audio frames) vs 96,150 bytes for 'I need help immediately'. Installed SAPI5 voices: only Microsoft David/Zira (en-US); OneCore adds only en-IN Ravi/Heera, which also produced 46-byte silence on Devanagari. The deleted line `speakText(text, ttsRate, ttsVolume, ttsLanguage || 'english')` was the only code path that ever attached hi-IN language metadata to an utterance. CLAUDE.md lists 'Bilingual: English + Hindi' as a core requirement and 'any regression in the emergency path is CRITICAL'.

findings.title: Backend connected but TTS engine dead = patient completely mute, no fallback, no error surfaced
findings.scenario: pyttsx3 fails to import (main.py:65-69 just logs a warning and the WebSocket server runs normally), or pyttsx3.init() throws in the worker thread (main.py:703-705 sets engine=None and continues), or the engine deadlocks in runAndWait (known pyttsx3 failure mode â€” the worker queue then backs up forever). In all three cases ws.isConnected stays true, so handleSpeak takes the ws.speak branch, the backend logs 'TTS not available. Would speak: ...' (main.py:738) and produces nothing, and the browser fallback NEVER fires. The patient â€” including pressing EMERGENCY â€” is totally voiceless with no on-screen indication. Before this commit, browser speechSynthesis fired on every utterance unconditionally, so a broken backend TTS still left the patient a working voice.
findings.evidence: handleSpeak (App.tsx:245-249): `if (ws.isConnected && ws.speak) { ws.speak(text); } else { speakText(...) }` â€” the fallback condition is socket connectivity, not TTS health. The backend _speak (main.py:2560-2566) sends no acknowledgement or failure message back to the client (it only calls self.tts.speak and log_speak), so the frontend has no way to detect that speech produced no audio. TTSEngine.speak with TTS_AVAILABLE=False: `_queue is None` so it falls to `if not self.engine: logger.warning(...); return` (main.py:737-739) â€” silent no-op while the WS connection reports healthy.

findings.title: Reconnect after a fallback utterance reintroduces double-speak, and the browser utterance is unstoppable
findings.line: 245-249 (no speechSynthesis.cancel() on the connected path); src/hooks/useWebSocket.tsx:501-510; src/screens/WebBrowsingScreen.tsx:1242, 2521
findings.scenario: Backend restarts mid-session (start-dev relaunch, Python crash â€” reconnect backoff is 1s on the first attempt, useWebSocket.tsx:504-509). During the gap the patient speaks the long bilingual emergency phrase or a sentence from the keyboard â€” browser speechSynthesis starts a multi-second utterance via the fallback. The socket reconnects ~1s later. The patient's next selection goes to ws.speak only, so the backend voice starts WHILE the browser utterance is still playing: two overlapping voices â€” the exact symptom this commit claims to fix. Worse, it cannot be stopped: the Stop buttons (WebBrowsingScreen.tsx:1242 'n-stop', 2521 'kb-stop') call only ws.stopSpeaking(), which stops pyttsx3, and window.speechSynthesis.cancel() now only ever runs inside speakText (App.tsx:146), which the connected path never reaches. The stale browser utterance plays to completion over everything.
findings.evidence: Pre-fix handleSpeak called speakText on EVERY utterance, and speakText begins with `window.speechSynthesis.cancel()` (App.tsx:146) â€” so any lingering browser speech was cancelled before each new utterance, bounding overlap. Post-fix, the connected branch (App.tsx:245-246) calls ws.speak(text) with no browser-engine cancel, and grep confirms speechSynthesis.cancel() exists nowhere else in src/ or electron/. ws.stopSpeaking is `send('stop_speaking')` (useWebSocket.tsx:609) which reaches only TTSEngine.stop (main.py:750-764) â€” backend-side only.

findings.title: Voice Settings panel (volume incl. '0 = muted', speech rate, TTS language) has zero audible effect whenever the backend is connected
findings.scenario: Caregiver opens Settings -> Voice Settings and sets Volume to 0 (the UI explicitly says '0 = muted') or lowers the speech rate for intelligibility. Nothing changes: every utterance still plays at the backend's hardcoded volume 1.0 and rate 150 WPM (main.py:690-691). Before this commit, the settings-honoring browser voice played on every single utterance (speakText applied ttsRate/ttsVolume per-utterance and its `volume <= 0` early-return at App.tsx:144 made mute work for that voice); after this commit, the configurable voice never plays while connected, so the entire panel is decorative â€” the patient hears fixed-rate, full-volume, English-voice-only speech and the slider/dropdown changes do nothing until the backend goes down.
findings.evidence: ws.speak sends only `{ text }` (useWebSocket.tsx:608) â€” ttsRate/ttsVolume/ttsLanguage are dropped on the connected path. The backend does implement 'set_tts_rate'/'set_tts_volume' handlers (main.py:1862-1863), but grep across src/ and electron/ shows ZERO call sites for ws.setTTSRate / ws.setTTSVolume â€” they are defined in useWebSocket.tsx:610-611 and never invoked, so the backend stays at its init defaults rate=150 / volume=1.0 forever. AppSettingsPanel.tsx:654-663 advertises 'Volume ... (0 = muted)' and a WPM rate slider whose values now reach no audio path while connected. (The backend ignoring these settings is pre-existing, but pre-fix the browser voice honoring them played on every utterance; this diff removes the only settings-honoring voice from connected sessions.)
